Hi everyone!

I’m trying to run the Morpheus example for DFP but I’m stuck. I’m following the instructions from the Github official repository (as referenced in GTC Developer Day 2023): Morpheus/examples/digital\_fingerprinting/production at branch-25.02 · nv-morpheus/Morpheus · GitHub However I’m running into a few issues. For those of you who have successfully run the DFP model, which instructions did you follow? There seems to be very little documentation available on this.

Thanks in advance!

Hi,  
Could you please provide more details about the specific issues you’re encountering while trying to build/run DFP using the instructions in the Morpheus GitHub repository? This information will help me assist you more effectively.

results.title: Morpheus8 Ruined My Skin: How To Avoid Complications
results.content:
But here’s the catch: Morpheus8 is not a one-size-fits-all treatment. It requires precise settings based on individual skin types, treatment areas, and goals. In the wrong hands, this powerful tool can lead to issues that may make someone feel like it’s ruined their skin rather than improved it.

### 

### What Can Go Wrong with Morpheus8?

Morpheus8 has a lot of potential benefits, but complications can arise if the treatment is not customized or performed correctly. Here are some things that can go wrong:

#### 1. Fat Loss [...] #### 1. Fat Loss

One of the more concerning complications from Morpheus8 is unwanted fat loss, particularly in areas like the cheeks, under-eye area, or temples. While Morpheus8 is designed to work on the skin’s surface and just beneath it, going too deep can result in damage to the fat layer, leading to a sunken, gaunt appearance.

This problem typically happens when the treatment settings, specifically the depth of the needles, are too aggressive for a particular area. For example, the skin on your lower face can tolerate deeper treatments, but areas like the forehead or temples need a lighter touch. If the wrong depth is used, it can lead to permanent fat loss, which can make you look older or gaunt instead of refreshed and youthful.

#### 2. Scarring [...] However, in my experience, when Morpheus8 is performed thoughtfully and conservatively, it can actually enhance facelift outcomes rather than hinder them. Properly applied, it helps improve overall skin health, thickens the dermis, and promotes stronger, more resilient collagen. These changes make the skin easier to manipulate during surgery and often lead to smoother, longer-lasting results.

However, these scabs are not permanent and shed as soon as the skin heals properly. Although it may take time for you to see the results if you have scabs, once the shedding is over, you will notice dramatic improvement.

Other common side effects can include temporary bruising, tenderness or soreness, and a occasionally pain for some patients. You can manage these symptoms with over-the-counter medications. In most cases, if you feel any irritation, it’s only mild and will stop soon after the treatment.

## What to do about the side effects

results.title: 5 Tips for Production Code Readiness - Hybrid Cloud Management and Automation | Morpheus
results.content:
For example, JavaScript is often minified, but this process can break the code if a developer missed the insertion of a standard semicolon somewhere. This oversight is possible because JavaScript has a feature to automatically insert missing semicolons at line breaks at runtime. This will hide the missing semicolon from a developer testing the code, but won’t hide it once the code is minified for production. A good linting tool can find this as well as a number of other things that could cause problems if not fixed.

A linting tool working inside of a code editor. Source:Treehouse.

## Use Code Reviews [...] testing can be used to help further ensure that everything is in good working order before being deployed to production.

With load testing, you find out at what point your system cannot handle any further simultaneous traffic or transactions such as large database queries. If a change to the code introduces enough of a slowdown from your previous load test, it is likely a good idea to take another look at the potential change to see if anything can be optimized.

QA testing is a way to allow one or more people to use the system as a potential user would. With this, any odd bugs can be found and fixed before an actual user is introduced to them, which helps avoid such things as unexpected hotfixes or rollbacks.

## Use Linting Tools for Non-compile Languages [...] ## Use Code Reviews

Having one or more other developers review the code before merging the changes into the master branch can be another helpful method of avoiding problems when moving to production. A human reviewer can oftentimes find optimizations or potentially bad practices in the code that can be remedied quickly and help ensure that those issues aren’t merged into the master branch.

When making sure your code is ready for production, all of these techniques can be extremely helpful for finding and fixing any issues before your production release.
